summary: Validated M4 stress evidence across rainy-season campaign cancellation, out-of-supply withdrawal consequences, AI wait/withdraw/target-change traces, siege outcomes, and 4000-row stress fixture browser smoke. Added a query-param web-shell stress fixture path and corresponding smoke coverage without widening gameplay scope.

tests_run.result: Working tree contained only four modified allowed files under apps/web and tests.

SIM-005 performance baseline passed on win32 x64 cpuCount=16 with runtime=node v24.13.1; days=3650; finalDay=3650; finalRevision=3650; dayThroughputPerSecond=13732; p50StepNanoseconds=58000; p95StepNanoseconds=130400; p99StepNanoseconds=200800; saveByteLength=9980; saveNanoseconds=1725100; loadNanoseconds=2705300.

Node and worker-compatible hashes matched for hello simulation (61e0ba1c), command/query (d275faa4), save/load (28074edd), and M4 replay (232f62ab) with engagement hash 334e0df0, siege hash ac35e97e, withdrawal hash 232f62ab, and postwarCandidateCount=1.
Vite production web build passed; output included the main shell and host-hash-canary entry points and a pre-existing chunk-size warning for large bundles.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic sim runner matched; 33 Vitest files passed with 239 tests; web Vitest passed.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/sim-core-m4-campaign-objectives.test.ts tests/sim-core-m4-withdrawal-postwar-handoff.test.ts tests/sim-core-m4-campaign-ai.test.ts tests/sim-core-m4-auto-engagement-siege.test.ts tests/sim-core-m4-march-supply-state.test.ts tests/client-core-m2-district-list.test.ts tests/ui-m2-district-list.test.tsx
tests_run.result: 7 files passed; 63 tests passed for focused M4 scenario evidence, including rainy-season cancellation, withdrawal consequences, AI trace, siege, and district-list pressure fixtures.

tests_run.command: pnpm test:e2e -- --grep "M4 stress fixture renders campaign planning and war report under 4000-row pressure"
tests_run.result: Playwright browser smoke passed on chromium for the 4000-row stress fixture, covering campaign planning workspace, district-list pressure counts, supply/risk text, and war-report rendering.

decisions: Kept the default web shell unchanged and added an opt-in query-param fixture path for stress validation only.
Reused existing M4 simulation and client read-model evidence instead of expanding gameplay, protocol, or production runtime scope.
Used a Playwright smoke against the same 4000-row pressure fixture to prove browser behavior under load.
risks: The stress fixture entry is an opt-in validation path; if product wants a permanent user-facing affordance, that should be handled in a separate scoped task.
The performance baseline is a single-machine measurement and should be treated as evidence for this environment, not a universal fleet benchmark.
requested_action: Review the M4 stress validation evidence against the task acceptance criteria and confirm whether the task is ready for QA acceptance.
